regions.name: Amman
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April when temperatures are moderate and ideal for exploring the capital's museums and Roman ruins.

regions.name: Dead Sea
regions.bestTimeToVisit: November to March to avoid extreme summer heat at the lowest point on Earth.

regions.name: Jericho Area
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April when temperatures are comfortable for visiting archaeological sites.

regions.name: Jerusalem Area
regions.bestTimeToVisit: April to May and September to October for pleasant weather and fewer crowds at holy sites.

regions.name: West Bank
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to November and March to April for mild temperatures and clear skies.

regions.name: Jordan Valley
regions.bestTimeToVisit: November to March when temperatures are moderate in this fertile lowland region.

regions.name: North Jordan
regions.bestTimeToVisit: April to May and September to October for pleasant weather in this agricultural region.

regions.name: Ajloun & Jerash
regions.bestTimeToVisit: March to May and September to November for ideal weather to explore Roman ruins.

regions.name: Amman Surroundings
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April for comfortable temperatures exploring nearby Bedouin villages and Roman sites.

regions.name: Salt
regions.bestTimeToVisit: September to November and March to May when temperatures are cool and pleasant.

regions.name: Madaba Area
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April for comfortable exploration of Byzantine mosaics and nearby archaeological sites.

regions.name: Central Jordan
regions.bestTimeToVisit: April to May and September to October for pleasant highland weather.

regions.name: Karak & Moab
regions.bestTimeToVisit: April to May and September to October for comfortable temperatures at these highland plateaus.

regions.name: Dead Sea South
regions.bestTimeToVisit: November to March to avoid extreme summer temperatures in this desert region.

regions.name: Petra & Wadi Musa
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April when temperatures are moderate for exploring the carved rock tombs.

regions.name: Southern Desert
regions.bestTimeToVisit: November to February for manageable temperatures in this arid desert landscape.

regions.name: Wadi Rum
regions.bestTimeToVisit: October to April for comfortable desert temperatures and clear starry nights.

regions.name: Aqaba
regions.bestTimeToVisit: November to March when sea temperatures are warm but air temperatures are moderate.

top10.name: Amman
top10.description: Jordan's vibrant capital city built on seven hills, featuring Roman theaters, Byzantine churches, and a thriving modern culture. It serves as the gateway to exploring the entire region and offers excellent museums, restaurants, and shopping.

top10.name: Petra
top10.description: An ancient Nabatean city carved into rose-red sandstone cliffs, Petra is one of the most iconic archaeological sites in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Treasury facade and the Monastery are must-see monuments that showcase extraordinary stone-carving craftsmanship from over 2,000 years ago.

top10.name: Dead Sea
top10.description: The lowest point on Earth at 1,410 feet below sea level, famous for its mineral-rich waters that allow visitors to float effortlessly and therapeutic mud. A unique natural wonder offering world-class spas and resorts.

top10.name: Wadi Rum
top10.description: A stunning desert landscape of red sand dunes and towering sandstone cliffs that has captivated visitors for centuries. The protected area offers jeep safaris, Bedouin camps, and pristine stargazing in one of the world's most dramatic natural environments.

top10.name: Jerash
top10.description: One of the best-preserved Roman cities outside Italy, featuring dramatic colonnaded streets, temples, and theaters set in a lush valley north of Amman. A testament to Roman provincial grandeur and engineering.

top10.name: Aqaba
top10.description: Jordan's only coastal city on the Red Sea, offering world-class diving and snorkeling opportunities among vibrant coral reefs. Located south of Amman, it provides beach resort facilities, water sports, and a gateway to nearby Wadi Rum and Petra.

top10.name: Madaba
top10.description: An ancient town renowned for its stunning Byzantine and Islamic mosaics, particularly the famous 6th-century map of the Holy Land in St. George's Church. The labyrinthine streets and numerous churches make it a cultural treasure for those interested in religious heritage.

top10.name: Wadi Musa
top10.description: The modern town adjacent to Petra that serves as the main gateway and accommodation hub for visitors exploring the archaeological site. It offers hotels, restaurants, shops, and cultural experiences while maintaining proximity to one of the world's greatest ancient ruins.

top10.name: Ajloun Castle
top10.description: A beautifully preserved 12th-century Islamic fortress perched on a hilltop with commanding views of the Jordan Valley, built to control trade routes and resist Crusader invasions. Located an hour north of Amman, it offers both historical significance and scenic mountain landscapes.

top10.name: Mount Nebo
top10.description: An ancient pilgrimage site situated 800 meters above the Jordan Valley, traditionally believed to be where Moses viewed the Promised Land before his death. The site features a Byzantine church, stunning panoramic views of the Dead Sea and Jordan River valley, and a fascinating mosaic floor.
